title: Depth-First Search
code:
module IntSet = Set.Make(Int)
module IntMap = Map.Make(Int)

type graph = int list IntMap.t

let dfs (g : graph) (start : int) : int list =
  let rec go node visited acc =
    if IntSet.mem node visited then (visited, acc)
    else
      let visited = IntSet.add node visited in
      let neighbors = IntMap.find_opt node g |> Option.value ~default:[] in
      let visited, acc = List.fold_left (fun (v, a) n -> go n v a) (visited, node :: acc) neighbors in
      (visited, acc)
  in
  snd (go start IntSet.empty []) |> List.rev
description: DFS using OCaml's functorized `Set` and `Map` modules. The recursive helper threads visited state through pure values — no mutation needed. `List.fold_left` iterates neighbors, accumulating both the visited set and the result.
